decisions.title: Approve Canvas V1 Feature Freeze for GA
decisions.context: Canvas features (ENG-9574 Make Copy, ENG-9233 Sharelinks, ENG-9442 Version History) are in flight. Need to decide if we freeze scope for GA or continue adding features, impacting 'Nail the Core' pillar.

arguments.for: Freezing scope allows faster GA, validates Canvas as decision-capture tool, aligns with North Star metric of decisions per customer
arguments.against: Missing features like Sync Video Panels (ENG-9825) may limit adoption, risk incomplete 'Embed Deeper' value

decisions.title: Prioritize Python Rules over Reactor MVP
decisions.context: ENG-10179 Reactor MVP and Python Rules are both strategic initiatives. Reactor enables 'Build the Moat' through automation, but Python Rules drives 'Nail the Core' by improving existing decision workflows.

arguments.for: Python Rules directly increases decisions captured per customer, serves existing user base, lower implementation risk
arguments.against: Reactor MVP creates differentiation moat, enables autonomous decision-making (strategic leap), delays risk future competitive pressure

decisions.title: Commit to FedRAMP Workspace Deployment Date
decisions.context: ENG-10156 provision AWS workspace for FedRAMP access is critical for Sift Edge gov deployment. Blocking 'Embed Deeper' in regulated markets. Compaction issues (ENG-10308, ENG-10237) suggest data infrastructure readiness concerns.
decisions.blockedBy: Compaction performance validation at gov-scale (ENG-10237) incomplete

arguments.for: Unblocks government customer expansion, validates Sift Edge in high-security context, major revenue opportunity
arguments.against: Infrastructure stability unknowns (compaction fragmentation ENG-10308), premature commit risks customer trust

decisions.title: Sunset Explore V1 UX by Q3 2026
decisions.context: DES-191 Explore User Preferences and ENG-10404 drag-and-drop Family stats suggest Explore V2 UX improvements ongoing. Setting sunset date for V1 forces migration completion, aligns with 'Nail the Core'.

arguments.for: Forces completion of V2 feature parity, reduces maintenance burden, concentrates user feedback on single experience
arguments.against: May alienate power users comfortable with V1, risk incomplete V2 features forcing rushed development

decisions.title: Allocate 20% Engineering Capacity to Technical Debt
decisions.context: Critical bugs (ENG-9978 Ghost events, ENG-9979 Report graph conditions, ENG-10298 Histogram filtering, ENG-10396 UDF React errors) accumulating. Quality issues threaten 'Nail the Core' and North Star metric.

arguments.for: Prevents quality erosion, maintains customer trust in core decisions, reduces future emergency firefighting
arguments.against: Slows feature velocity on Canvas/Explore V2, may miss competitive windows, harder to demonstrate progress to stakeholders

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load (80% of week) constraining strategic decision-making time. 3 one-on-ones suggest firefighting/unblocking mode rather than proactive planning.
alerts: Only 2 focus blocks scheduled today - insufficient for deep work on strategy decisions
22 meetings this week exceed healthy threshold (target: 15), recommend calendar audit
Zero team syncs visible on Canvas/Explore V2 progress - alignment risk
